_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1 Symphony pack. Built fresh 2026-06-04 during the careful-not-clever one-pack-per-session pass. Primary-source verification against Wikipedia ship article, Royal Caribbean press releases, royalcaribbean.com FAQ, current 2026 pricing sources. No claim cites another voyage pack in this repo (Copy-Propagation rule).
verification_session: Symphony first-pack-of-the-batch session per operator instruction 'one at a time, careful not clever, commit, then proceed in a new session.' All factual corrections from the earlier RCL fact-check audit (commits referenced inline) applied; voice-audit v2.3.0 doctrine applied; sidecar built fresh.

length_ft_m.notes: Corrected from prior pack value of 1,184 ft / 361 m during this audit pass.

builder.notes: Pack body does not currently name the builder; sidecar records for completeness.

class.value: Oasis-class, fourth of six (Oasis 2009, Allure 2010, Harmony 2016, Symphony 2018, Wonder 2022, Utopia 2024)
class.notes: Pack's prior 'third of five' was wrong. Corrected to 'fourth of six' with full sister-list.

sister_ships.value: Oasis of the Seas (2009), Allure of the Seas (2010), Harmony of the Seas (2016), Wonder of the Seas (2022), Utopia of the Seas (2024)

largest_at_launch.value: Yes — world's largest cruise ship at March 2018 launch; held title until Wonder of the Seas took it in January 2022. Now fourth-largest Oasis-class (Wonder, Utopia, plus the Icon-class ships are larger).

godparent.value: Carlos PenaVega + Alexa PenaVega + their 17-month-old son Ocean (the first 'godfamily' to christen a cruise ship in industry history — Royal Caribbean broke the traditional single-godmother convention with this 3-person designation)
godparent.notes: The PenaVegas got engaged on Oasis and honeymooned on Allure; Carlos had sailed Royal Caribbean for ~25 years before being named. Pack body does not currently mention the christening or godfamily; sidecar records for completeness; recommended for future inclusion in Section 1.

date.value: November 2018 (at PortMiami, on Symphony's first arrival to her US homeport)

selection_mechanism.value: Royal Caribbean chose the PenaVega family as the first-ever 'godfamily' in cruise christening history — a deliberate evolution of the traditional godmother role to include both parents and their child. Carlos had been sailing Royal for ~25 years; the family had ship-engagement and ship-honeymoon history with the line.

date.notes: Symphony was based in the Mediterranean for her first summer before repositioning to PortMiami in November 2018 for North American service.

route.value: Inaugural season in the Mediterranean (Barcelona base); repositioned to PortMiami November 2018 for Caribbean service

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $18.50/person/day in standard staterooms; $21.00/person/day in suites (effective November 1, 2024)
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Pack's prior '$16/person/day' was one rate cycle out of date. Corrected at six locations in the pack body (Section 2 Day 8 tipping subsection; Section 5 budget table + realistic mid-range; Section 8 tipping subsection; Section 11 surprise list).

wine_alcohol_policy.value: One (1) sealed 750ml bottle of wine or champagne per guest of drinking age at embarkation (per ADULT, not per stateroom). $15 corkage if consumed in public areas (no fee in stateroom). Boxed wine prohibited.
wine_alcohol_policy.notes: Pack's prior 'per stateroom' framing dated to a pre-March-2023 policy. Corrected to 'per adult of drinking age' with the corkage detail and the 'a 2-adult cabin can bring 2 bottles' clarification.

drink_package_pricing.value: Deluxe Beverage Package $77-95/day/person on Oasis-class ships typical; dynamic pricing varies. Pre-cruise booking 10-40% cheaper than onboard.
drink_package_pricing.notes: Pack's prior '$80-95/day' floor was high. Corrected to $77/day floor with note about dynamic pricing.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry; Royal Caribbean reserves DIY passenger laundry for world cruises ONLY. Full-service via cabin steward (per-item or bag-special) is the only option on regular itineraries.
laundry_availability.source: Pack-level policy documented in voice-audit corpus; RCL's world-cruise marketing is the public-facing reference.
laundry_availability.notes: Pack body already has the correct text in Section 8 (Laundry on board subsection). No fix needed; sidecar records.

app_naming.value: Royal Caribbean app (includes in-app Royal iQ chat messaging feature)
app_naming.notes: Pack uses 'Cruise Compass' (RCL's daily program — correct), 'Cruise Planner' (RCL's pre-cruise booking system — correct), and 'Royal Caribbean app' (the unified app — correct). No fix needed.

casino_w2g_threshold.value: $2,000 effective January 1, 2026 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act); previously $1,200
casino_w2g_threshold.source: Pack-cited; verified by IRS form documentation in industry coverage
casino_w2g_threshold.notes: Pack body already has the correct figure with the bill name. Verified accurate as of 2026-06-04.

port_prices._comment: Symphony port-price claims with current source. Verify per sailing if booked >60 days out.

roatan_west_bay_beach_taxi.value: ~$6/person each way from Mahogany Bay (~25 min taxi)
roatan_west_bay_beach_taxi.source: Multiple Roatán cruise-port guides + Mahogany Bay Taxi Association (MBTA) posted rates
roatan_west_bay_beach_taxi.notes: Worth re-verifying per sailing; taxi rates posted at pier.

cozumel_pier_snorkel_tour.source: Multiple Cozumel cruise-port guides; pier-vendor pricing varies

largest_cruise_ship_at_launch.claim: Symphony was the world's largest cruise ship at her March 2018 launch and held the title until Wonder of the Seas took it in January 2022
largest_cruise_ship_at_launch.disposition: sourced and currently-accurate (corrected from 'still ranks in the top tier' soft claim to the date-specific superseded-by-Wonder framing)

daredevils_peak_tallest_north_america.claim: Daredevil's Peak at 135 feet — the tallest waterslide in North America per Royal's marketing
daredevils_peak_tallest_north_america.disposition: sourced and softened (was 'world's tallest at the time of build' — that claim is wrong per Royal's own marketing which says Caribbean/North America tallest, not world's)

thrill_waterpark_slide_count.claim: 14 slides at Thrill Waterpark (corrected from pack's prior '13 slides')

studio_b_regulation_size_REMOVED.claim: (REMOVED) Pack's prior claim of 'regulation-size rink' for Studio B
studio_b_regulation_size_REMOVED.notes: Studio B on Oasis-class is smaller than NHL regulation (200×85 ft) and Olympic regulation (200×100 ft) per cruise-industry sources; no Royal Caribbean source claims regulation dimensions. Removed the 'regulation-size' qualifier; kept 'full ice rink at sea' as the defensible claim.

penavega_first_godfamily.claim: First 'godfamily' to christen a cruise ship in industry history
penavega_first_godfamily.disposition: sourced (not currently in pack body; documented in sidecar for future inclusion if the operator wants the christening detail)

specialty_dining_list.value: Chops Grille (steakhouse), 150 Central Park (the standout), Hooked Seafood, Izumi (sushi/hibachi), Sabor (Mexican), Wonderland (theatrical), Jamie's Italian (when in service), Coastal Kitchen (suites only)

included_dining.value: Main Dining Room (three-deck), Windjammer buffet, Café Promenade, Boardwalk Dog House, Sorrento's pizza, Solarium Bistro, El Loco Fresh, room service breakfast continental

studio_b.value: Center Ice Rink ('Studio B') — ice-skating venue with two-tiered show lounge seating ~1,000 guests; smaller than NHL/Olympic regulation rinks but a real ice-rink experience at sea

aquatheater.value: Outdoor amphitheater in the Boardwalk neighborhood; signature HiRO production show (high-energy dive + acrobatics); reservation recommended
aquatheater.notes: Pack mentions HiRO as the headliner. Show titles rotate per refurbishment cycle; confirm in Cruise Compass.

ultimate_abyss.value: 10-story dry slide at the aft of the ship (Deck 16 to Deck 6); the world's tallest slide at sea per Royal's marketing
ultimate_abyss.notes: Pack body uses '10-story slide' — accurate. The 'world's tallest at sea' superlative is Royal's marketing; not added to pack body.

neighborhoods.value: Seven 'neighborhoods': Royal Promenade, Central Park, Boardwalk, Pool & Sports Zone, Vitality Spa & Fitness, Entertainment Place, Youth Zone

drydocks.value: Most recent: March 2023 at Cádiz, Spain (Navantia) — technical refresh and routine maintenance
drydocks.notes: Pack body mentions 'Cádiz, Spain — technical refresh, hull repaint, minor amenity updates'. 'Hull repaint' specific is not independently verified — softening to 'technical refresh and routine maintenance' is the defensible framing. Pack body not updated this pass; flag for future revision.

ports_of_call.value: Typical Western Caribbean 7-night from Miami: Day 1 PortMiami embark Sat → Day 2 Sea → Day 3 Roatán → Day 4 Costa Maya → Day 5 Cozumel → Day 6 Sea → Day 7 Perfect Day at CocoCay → Day 8 PortMiami debark Sat
ports_of_call.source: Pack body Section 2; verifiable in Royal Caribbean Cruise Planner per booking
ports_of_call.notes: Pack appropriately frames this as 'varies by sailing — confirm yours'. Day order and arrival/departure times can vary.

saturday_to_saturday.value: Symphony's Western Caribbean itinerary runs Saturday-to-Saturday from PortMiami

voice_audit._note: Voice_audit block for v0.1 Symphony, built fresh during the one-pack-per-session careful-not-clever pass.
voice_audit.audit_scope_detail: 992 lines of admin/voyage-packs/v0.1-symphony-western-caribbean-7n.md, from cover Welcome through Closing.

machine_tells.banned_cruise_vocab_notes: 'world-class' on L222 (Cozumel reef) corrected in commit 26b1ac9d to named reef systems + national-park anchor. No remaining hits.
machine_tells.generic_ai_tells_notes: Earlier scan flagged 47 hits for 'Symphony' — all proper-noun ship-name references, all false positives. Filtered out.
machine_tells.antithetical_stacking_notes: L23 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' — within the v2.3.0 triplet-of-absences cap of one per top-level document.

templated_section_headers.notes: Pre-fix: 'The honest read on [port]:' templated header on Roatán L188, Costa Maya L206, Cozumel L222, CocoCay L256 (4 instances = above v2.3.0 threshold of 3). Post-fix: Cozumel L222 retained as the one signature instance; Roatán → 'Roatán — where the excursion choice matters most'; Costa Maya → 'Costa Maya — the easiest of the three Mexican ports'; CocoCay → 'CocoCay — a manufactured beach day, well-executed'. Mechanical template signal removed.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 10 'honest/honestly' instances exceeded the v2.3.0 threshold of 5. Post-fix: 2 instances retained — 'The honest read on Cozumel' (brand signature for the one signature port-day framing) + 'The honest answer' (verdict-delivering line after detailed drink-package math, lands cleanly). Reduced: Day-1 mistake header, 'Honest call' inline → 'Practical call', 'The honest cost' → 'The real cost', 'The honest baseline' → 'The baseline', 'Five honest options' → 'Five options'.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING — no first-person attestation in 992 lines
first_person_attestation_with_date.colophon_disclosure_escape: NOT INVOKED (operator declined the escape mechanism on 2026-06-04 for the Anthem pack, the decision is project-wide: do not propose colophon-disclosure for any pack). Path forward: lived-grade Layer 3 counter-signal anchors in subsequent revisions when the operator has sailed (or has access to logbook entries from cruisers who sailed) this specific itinerary.

locations: L188 (Roatán): 'It's also the port where ship-sponsored excursions are noticeably more expensive than independently arranged tours. If you have an opinion about that tradeoff, this is where it matters most.' — honest limitation about excursion-cost framing
L206 (Costa Maya): 'It's also the least "authentic Mexico" of the three — feels more like a resort than a port town. That's not a value judgment; it's information.' — honest qualifier
L256 (CocoCay): 'CocoCay — a manufactured beach day, well-executed.' — names the manufactured-ness upfront

examples: Mahogany Bay vs Coxen Hole pier distinction (Roatán)
Three Cozumel pier names (Punta Langosta, International, Puerta Maya)
$6 taxi to West Bay Beach with 25-min framing
Cozumel taxi specifics (Sindicato de Choferes Taxistas de Cozumel, white-with-red-stripe markings)
Mahogany Bay Taxi Association (MBTA) for Roatán
Mercado Municipal (San Miguel, 15-min walk from Punta Langosta)

real_numbers.verdict: strong — ship specs, prices in $, distances, deck numbers, times, percentages

examples: Palancar / Columbia / Santa Rosa Wall reef systems (Cozumel)
Sindicato de Choferes Taxistas de Cozumel
PenaVega family (godfamily, in sidecar not pack)
Specific Brickell + downtown Miami hotel-day-room mentions
Sindicato de Choferes Taxistas de Cozumel
Allianz Global Assistance, Travelex, Travel Guard, IMG, World Nomads (insurance providers)
named_real_specifics.note: Research-grade entities present in volume. Lived-grade anchors (named crew members, specific date attestations) absent.

voice_audit.absent_markers_drift_indicator: First-person attestation absent (1 marker missing). Below the v2.3.0 'three or more must-be-present markers missing' Medium-trigger threshold. Honest limitation + from-the-pier specificity + real numbers + named specifics + plain copulas + compressed declarative chains all present.

cluster_detection._framework: AI-Authorship Cluster Detection v3 (adapted from ken voice-audit)

signals: After the templated-header fix and the 'honest' crutch reduction, no Layer 1 semantic-placeholder or broad-authority-claim patterns survive in the pack body. Specific named anchors (reef systems, taxi associations, neighborhood names, deck numbers) provide concreteness throughout.
Stock cruise-content phrases: 'calm by design' (L23), 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' (L23) — brand phrases, within v2.3.0 single-instance allowance for triplet-of-absences
Mechanical structural patterns: pre-cruise countdown section uses formulaic timeframe headings (90/60/30/14/7/2 days before) — within corpus-native countdown pattern but worth noting
Named entities (research-grade) volume: 18+ named operators, associations, products
Localized claims with bounded scope: 'closed-loop' federal cruise rules cited specifically; Quintana Roo UTC-5 year-round observation
Specific verifiable details: PenaVega first-godfamily history (sidecar), Cádiz March 2023 drydock, IRS W-2G $2,000 threshold with bill name
Authorial hedging that names the limit of what's claimable: 'confirm with the cruise line', 'varies by sailing', 'verify in your Cruise Compass' patterns throughout
Specific verifiable Royal Caribbean policy citations (linked FAQ, cancellation rules, $15 corkage)
Friction acknowledgment: the budget breakdown openly admits 'a $2,000 cruise fare becomes ~$4,000 at the door'
Contrarian framing on multiple points: the drink-package math says 'pay-as-you-go is more honest', the Royal Promenade gets a deliberate undercut ('embarkation-day chaos peaks')
Specific timezone behavior observations (Roatán CST year-round, Quintana Roo never-DST)
Specific 2026-dated regulatory updates (W-2G threshold January 1 2026, REAL ID enforcement May 7 2025)
Honest body-overpromise hedge: seasickness section says 'Most cruisers... will not get meaningfully seasick' rather than 'you won't' — appropriate population-level framing

cluster_detection.scoring_walkthrough: 0 Layer 1 signals + 2 Layer 2 supporting signals + 11 Layer 3 counter-signals. Per the v2.3.0 cluster scoring formula: '0 Layer 1 → likely human regardless of Layer 2.' Counter-signals are dominantly research-grade rather than lived-grade (no first-person attestation, no named crew member, no specific date sailings). However, the structural depth, the specific regulatory citations, the contrarian framings, and the absence of any imagined-experience-on-reader's-behalf prose place this pack solidly in the human-likely territory. Verdict: likely_human.
cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Post-fix: zero Layer 1 strong signals; Layer 2 supporting signals within v2.3.0 single-instance allowances; 11 Layer 3 counter-signals (research-grade plus a handful of regulatory-citation and contrarian-framing signals that read native to the corpus). Cluster math: 0 Layer 1 → likely_human regardless of Layer 2. The lived-grade Layer 3 anchor is missing (no first-person attestation), but no individual signal pulls strongly enough toward likely_ai to override. Pack reads as research synthesis written competently, not as AI-template prose.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no 'perfect for', no 'must', no closing pitch. The 'Soli Deo Gloria' close is the brand's doctrinal signature, not promotional.
voice_audit.specificity_check: pass — specific to Symphony (the seven-neighborhood structure, specific deck-cabin recommendations, Studio B, Two70-less context, Ultimate Abyss). Specific to Western Caribbean (Cozumel reef names, Roatán pier distinction, Quintana Roo timezone).
voice_audit.cadence_check: pass — varied sentence length, varied paragraph length, em-dashes within cap.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Cluster verdict likely_human + zero Layer 1 signals + must-be-present markers mostly present (only first-person attestation absent, which is below the 3+ missing threshold for Medium escalation) + machine-tell counts at or near zero post-fix. Pack passes the Six-Axis Scan after the 2026-06-04 corrections. The Anthem-historical-anchor escalation trigger (reader feedback citing 'reads like AI') has not fired for this pack. The Pack does retain the 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' brand triplet and one signature 'honest read on Cozumel' phrasing — both within v2.3.0 doctrine caps.
recommended_followup_edits: Add christening context (PenaVega family, Nov 2018 PortMiami, first 'godfamily' in cruise history) to Section 1 — a natural narrative anchor for the ship section and a Layer 3 counter-signal opportunity
Soften Section 8 drydock claim from 'hull repaint, minor amenity updates' to 'technical refresh and routine maintenance' (the 'hull repaint' specific is not independently verified)

When a verified contributor has sailed Symphony Western Caribbean, insert a first-person attestation in Section 1 or Section 2 — that's the path to converting cluster verdict's research-grade counter-signals to lived-grade
Consider adding the Saint-Nazaire (Chantiers de l'Atlantique) builder detail to At-a-Glance for class/build completeness
voice_audit.outstanding_drift_notes: Audit is comprehensive single-pass under voice-audit v2.3.0 + original-research doctrine. No orchestra-pass extension run this session (the Anthem orchestra-pass pattern is documented in the doctrine; per-pack orchestra runs are optional and can follow if a reader-feedback escalation fires).
